description_long: This is a classic pipe wrench featuring a robust metallic head and an elongated wooden handle. The head is constructed from a heavy, weathered metal, showing intricate mechanical details such as an adjustable jaw and a knurled adjustment nut. The jaws are thick and serrated, designed for gripping pipes or fittings securely. The handle is cylindrical, made of wood with a smooth, slightly worn finish, capped with a metallic end. The wrench exhibits signs of use, with visible scuffs and a patina on the metal, while the wood shows some minor wear but remains intact. This tool is typical of those found in urban garages, workshops, or maintenance settings.
description: A sturdy pipe wrench with a weathered metal head and a smooth wooden handle, featuring an adjustable serrated jaw. The tool shows signs of use, with scuffs and a patina on the metal and minor wear on the wood.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular end of the metal head and the alignment of the jaws, with the wooden handle receding into the background.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length of the wrench, the detailed adjustment mechanism, and the smooth wooden handle.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the rounded end of the wooden handle and the back of the adjustment mechanism.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile displaying the serrated jaws, adjustment nut, and the length of the wooden handle.
attribute_handle_grip_pattern: smooth wood with minor wear, no distinct grip pattern
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Visible scuffs and patina on the metal head, minor wear on the wooden handle
